title: Agelanthus oehleri
intro: Agelanthus oehleri, also known as the 'yellow mistletoe', is a hemiparasitic plant that grows on the branches of various host trees, obtaining its nutrients through a symbiotic relationship. Traditionally, Agelanthus oehleri has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Agelanthus oehleri is often used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Agelanthus oehleri is a species of flowering plant in the family Loranthaceae. It belongs to the order Santalales and the clade Santalanae. The plant is a hemiparasitic member of the Loranthaceae family, which includes other mistletoe species. Agelanthus oehleri is further classified as a member of the genus Agelanthus, which is a small group of flowering plants found in tropical Africa. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and phylogenetic relationships.

parts: Agelanthus oehleri is a species of flowering plant in the family Loranthaceae, commonly known as mistletoes. preparations: Agelanthus oehleri is a parasitic plant traditionally used in herbal medicine. In some parts of Africa, it is employed to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. The plant's exact mechanism of action and efficacy are not well-documented, and more research is needed to understand its full potential. Herbal preparations of Agelanthus oehleri are often made from its roots, leaves, or entire plant, and may be consumed as teas, infusions, or dried extracts. However, due to the plant's toxicity and potential side effects, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies derived from Agelanthus oehleri.
